design_notes: Rising spiral radius decreases with height (tighter upper portion).
Final section rotates outward and slightly upward away from spiral center.
Maintain tangent continuity and constant hollow cross-section until final taper.

draft.rationale: Completing the 'rising_spiral' goal using the 'relative_to_target' frame. To address curvature rejections from previous attempts, I've added smoothing waypoints to ease the transition from the vertical target port axis into the spiral. [0,0,1.5] provides an initial axial extension, and [1.25,0,3] softens the lateral shift toward the first anchor [10,0,5]. I've also added a midpoint [22.5,11.25,11.25] between the first and second anchors to distribute the turning radius effectively. The final_tangent points outward and upward, consistent with the design notes.

issues.message: A curved/tapered envelope may intersect; FreeCAD Boolean evidence is authoritative.
A curved/tapered envelope may intersect; FreeCAD Boolean evidence is authoritative.

draft.rationale: Creating the initial 40mm straight section along the +X axis as specified in the 'straight_inlet' goal.

I am completing the 'upward_bend' goal by using a circular_arc turn. The target port M1.out has an axis of [1, 0, 0] (+X). To achieve a 90-degree turn toward the +Z direction as specified in the goal, I perform a positive 90-degree sweep about the [0, -1, 0] axis. This axis is derived from the cross product of the inlet tangent [1, 0, 0] and the target direction [0, 0, 1]. All parameters use the provided enum-safe string representations.

observations.message: A curved/tapered envelope may intersect; FreeCAD Boolean evidence is authoritative.

draft.rationale: I am initiating the rising spiral spline route from port M2.out. The waypoints are authored in the 'relative_to_target' frame as specified in the goal, matching the contract's requirement for a radius that decreases with height. I set the final_tangent to roughly align with the slope between the last two waypoints (chord vector [-11, -20, 5]) using available schema values [-1, -2, 0.5] which preserves the inward and upward trajectory.

M3.curvature_repair_hint: The curvature peak is near the spline inlet. Add one or more smoothing waypoints after the inlet along initial_tangent before turning toward the required anchors; changing only final_tangent cannot repair an inlet-local peak.

draft.rationale: The previous attempt failed due to a curvature peak at the inlet where the spline immediately transitioned from the vertical target axis to the first required anchor. Per the repair hint, I have added two smoothing waypoints along the initial tangent ([0,0,1.5] and [0,0,3]) to ensure a gradual transition. I included all required waypoints in their original order. The final tangent is directed outward from the spiral's approximate center and slightly upward as required by the design notes.

M3.curvature_repair_hint: The curvature peak is near authored path point index 2. Preserve required anchors but spread this direction change over additional, well-separated waypoints before and after it.

draft.rationale: The previous attempt failed due to a curvature peak at waypoint index 2 ([10, 0, 5] relative). Per the repair hint, I have added smoothing waypoints both before and after the offending anchor to spread the direction change: [0, 0, 3] and [5, 0, 3.75] are inserted before the turn starts, and [20, 10, 10] is inserted after to bridge the transition to the next anchor. This maintains all required points in order while avoiding sharp curvature. The final tangent is set to follow the outward/upward exit trajectory specified in the design notes.

M3.curvature_repair_hint: The curvature peak is near authored path point index 1. Preserve required anchors but spread this direction change over additional, well-separated waypoints before and after it.

draft.rationale: To resolve the curvature violation at the first waypoint, I am using the required 'relative_to_target' waypoint frame and inserting smoothing waypoints (1.25, 0, 2.5) and (3.75, 0, 3.75). These points help the spline transition gradually from the port's vertical axis [0,0,1] to the first required waypoint [10,0,5]. The final tangent [-1, 0, 0.5] is chosen to satisfy the contract of rotating outward from the spiral center (roughly at 15,20) and slightly upward.

M3.curvature_repair_hint: The curvature peak is near the spline inlet. Add one or more smoothing waypoints after the inlet along initial_tangent before turning toward the required anchors; changing only final_tangent cannot repair an inlet-local peak.

draft.rationale: Previous attempts failed due to a curvature peak at the spline inlet. To resolve this, I have added three smoothing waypoints in the 'relative_to_target' frame before the first required goal waypoint [10, 0, 5]. These points (0,0,1.5), (1.25, 0, 3), and (3.75, 0, 4) extend the axial segment from the port and introduce the transverse direction change more gradually over the limited 5mm vertical height. The final tangent is directed away from the spiral center (roughly at 15,20) and slightly upward, satisfying the design notes.

M3.curvature_repair_hint: The curvature peak is near authored path point index 1. Preserve required anchors but spread this direction change over additional, well-separated waypoints before and after it.

draft.rationale: The previous attempts failed due to curvature peaks at the spline inlet. To resolve this, I am reducing the number of smoothing waypoints and ensuring they are better separated to allow the spline solver to create a smoother, larger-radius transition. I've replaced the clustered points with a single intermediate waypoint [2.5, 0, 1.5] before the first required goal anchor [10, 0, 5]. This point initiates the turn towards the spiral earlier and more gradually over the limited 5mm vertical height available. The final_tangent [-1, 0, 0.5] satisfies the design requirement to rotate 'outward' from the spiral center (roughly at X=15, Y=20) and 'slightly upward'.

bop_errors: <depth-truncated>

M3.curvature_repair_hint: The curvature peak is near the spline inlet. Add one or more smoothing waypoints after the inlet along initial_tangent before turning toward the required anchors; changing only final_tangent cannot repair an inlet-local peak.

draft.rationale: Previous attempts were rejected due to high curvature peaks (small bend radii) at the beginning of the spline. To resolve this, I have implemented the repair suggestion by adding a smoothing waypoint [0, 0, 1.5] directly along the initial tangent axis to stabilize the sweep start. I also added well-separated intermediate smoothing waypoints [3.75, 0, 3] and [22.5, 11.25, 10] to distribute the sharp direction changes required by the immutable goal anchors over larger arc lengths. The final tangent [-1, 0, 0.5] satisfies the design requirement to rotate outward from the spiral center and slightly upward.

M3.curvature_repair_hint: The curvature peak is near authored path point index 1. Preserve required anchors but spread this direction change over additional, well-separated waypoints before and after it.

draft.rationale: The previous attempts failed due to curvature peaks at the beginning of the spline, where the centerline turns sharply from the vertical target axis to the first required anchor [10, 0, 5]. To resolve this, I have added two smoothing waypoints along and near the initial axis: [0, 0, 2] and [2.5, 0, 4]. These points distribute the direction change over a larger arc length before reaching the first goal point. I also added a smoothing waypoint [22.5, 11.25, 10] between the first and second goal points to maintain a consistent spiral curvature. The final_tangent [-1, -0.5, 0.5] is chosen to rotate 'outward' from the estimated spiral center (roughly at 15, 20) and 'slightly upward' as specified in the design notes.

M3.curvature_repair_hint: The curvature peak is near authored path point index 1. Preserve required anchors but spread this direction change over additional, well-separated waypoints before and after it.

draft.rationale: To address the recurring geometry validation failure at the start of the spline, I added two smoothing waypoints ([1.25, 0, 2.5] and [3.75, 0, 4] in the relative frame) to ease the transition from the vertical inlet axis [0, 0, 1] to the first required waypoint [10, 0, 5]. This reduces the initial curvature peak by spreading the radial displacement across a longer axial distance. I used the relative_to_target frame as specified in the goal and kept the final tangent aligned with the CCW spiral flow and the start of the next S-curve goal.

M3.curvature_repair_hint: The curvature peak is near the spline inlet. Add one or more smoothing waypoints after the inlet along initial_tangent before turning toward the required anchors; changing only final_tangent cannot repair an inlet-local peak.

draft.rationale: The previous attempt failed due to a curvature peak at the inlet. I have added a strict axial smoothing waypoint at [0, 0, 1.5] and a gradual transitional waypoint at [1.25, 0, 3] to align the centerline with the initial tangent before engaging the first anchor. I also added a mid-segment waypoint at [15, 5, 7.5] to smooth the transition between the first and second required anchors. These choices follow the FreeCAD repair hint to spread the direction change over more waypoints starting immediately after the inlet.

M3.curvature_repair_hint: The curvature peak is near the spline outlet. Add one or more smoothing waypoints before the terminal point aligned with final_tangent; changing only an inlet-side point cannot repair an outlet-local peak.

draft.rationale: Previous attempts failed due to a curvature peak at the spline outlet (index 7). Following the FreeCAD repair hint, I added two smoothing waypoints before the terminal point ([6, 35, 25] and [1.25, 22.5, 30] in the relative frame) to ease the transition between the third and fourth required anchors. I also adjusted the final tangent to [-1, -1.25, 0.5] to better align with the natural flow of the tightening spiral, reducing the bending strain at the terminal port.
